common.serviceName: Check in with your probation officer

phaseBanner.html: This is a new service – your feedback (opens in a new tab) will help us to improve it.
phaseBanner.htmlNoLink: This is a new service – your feedback will help us to improve it.

visuallyHidden.mentalHealthTextBox: Selecting this option will reveal a textbox

index.heading: Check in with your probation officer
index.intro: You can use this service to check in with your probation officer as part of your probation. Your probation officer will have signed you up for this service during a face-to-face appointment.

beforeStart.heading: Before you start

items.details: your name and date of birth

beforeStart.uk: You will not be able to use this service to check in if you are outside of the UK.

whatToDo.heading: What you need to do
whatToDo.intro: As part of this service you will need to:

items.questions: answer a set of questions relating to how you are and if you need any help
items.camera: give permission to access your device’s camera
items.video: record a short video of yourself so we can check it’s you using the service

whatToDo.videoUse: We will use your video to confirm who you are. We will do this by comparing it with the photo your probation officer took when they signed you up.
whatToDo.shareAnswers: The answers to your questions will be sent to your probation officer to review.

help.heading: If you need help
help.intro: If you have difficulty using this service, or you do not have access to everything you need, you should contact your probation officer and let them know.
help.guidance: You can learn more about online check ins in our guidance.

verify.title: Personal details
verify.heading: Personal details
verify.intro: We will use your personal details to make sure you are signed up to use this service.

noMatchFound.title: No match found
noMatchFound.intro: We were unable to verify your identity on our system. Check your information for any errors and try again.

table.caption: Details that were used to verify your identity

help.heading: Need help?
help.intro: If your information is correct and you are still unable to verify your identity, you must contact your probation officer.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.heading: Page not found
notFound.checkAddressTyped: If you typed the web address, check it is correct.
notFound.checkAddressPasted: If you pasted the web address, check you copied the entire address.
notFound.linkOutOfDate: If you clicked on a link to get here, the link may be out of date.
notFound.contactOfficer: If you need help, contact your probation officer.

expired.title: Your check in link has expired
expired.intro: Contact your probation officer to request a new link.

timeout.title: Your check in has been reset
timeout.intro: For your security, your answers have been deleted.

confirmation.title: Check in completed
confirmation.sent: We have sent you a confirmation message.

whatNext.heading: What happens next?
whatNext.intro: Your video and answers have been shared with your probation officer. They will contact you if there are any concerns with the information you’ve entered, or if you requested contact.
whatNext.reminder: We will remind you when it is time for your next check in.

feedback.heading: Have your say about online check ins
feedback.intro: Help us to improve check ins by answering a few questions about your experience.

mentalHealth.title: How have you been feeling since we last spoke?
mentalHealth.legend: How have you been feeling since we last spoke?
mentalHealth.hint: Think about things like if you have noticed a change in your mood and what may have caused this.
mentalHealth.veryWellLabel: Tell us why you are very well (optional)
mentalHealth.wellLabel: Tell us why you are well (optional)
mentalHealth.okLabel: Tell us why you are OK (optional)
mentalHealth.notGreatLabel: Tell us why you are not great (optional)
mentalHealth.strugglingLabel: Tell us why you are struggling (optional)
mentalHealth.insetHtml: If you need to speak to someone urgently about how you are feeling, check the NHS website for help (opens in new tab).

errors.required: Select how you have been feeling since we last spoke

assistance.title: Is there anything you need support with or want to let us know about?
assistance.legend: Is there anything you need support with or want to let us know about?
assistance.hint: This could be anything you're worrying about, struggling with or just want to let us know.

options.noHelp: No, I do not need any support

labels.mentalHealth: Tell us what you want us to know about mental health (optional)
labels.alcohol: Tell us what you want us to know about alcohol (optional)
labels.drugs: Tell us what you want us to know about drugs (optional)
labels.money: Tell us what you want us to know about money (optional)
labels.housing: Tell us what you want us to know about housing (optional)
labels.employmentEdu: Tell us what you want us to know about employment and education (optional)
labels.supportSystem: Tell us what you want us to know about your relationships (optional)
labels.other: Tell us what you want us to know about (optional)

errors.required: Select what you need help with or select 'No, I do not need any support'

callback.title: Would you like us to contact you about anything before your next appointment?
callback.legend: Would you like us to contact you about anything before your next appointment?
callback.hint: This could be something you've already asked for help with or something new.
callback.detailsLabel: Tell us what you need to talk about, for example, something you're struggling with or need more information about (optional)
callback.inset: If this is urgent, you should contact your probation officer yourself. If there is an emergency, call 999.

errors.required: Enter your answer to the question

videoResult.match: We have confirmed this is you
videoResult.noMatch: We cannot confirm this is you

identityResult.match: We have confirmed this is you
identityResult.noMatch: We cannot confirm this is you
identityResult.notLiveNoMatch: There's been a problem with your identity check

complete.confirm: I confirm that, to the best of my knowledge, the details I have provided are correct. I understand that this information will be shared with my probation officer.

inform.title: Confirm your identity
inform.intro: Next, we need to record a 5-second video of you on this device. You do not need to speak in the video. Anything you say will not be recorded.
inform.comparison: We’ll use your video to confirm who you are. We’ll do this by comparing your video with the photo your probation officer took when they signed you up. This video will only be used to confirm your identity.
inform.permissions: You’ll need to accept permissions to record a video on your device.
inform.rulesIntro: Before you record your video, make sure:

rules.litArea: you are in a well lit area
rules.plainBackground: you are in front of a plain background
rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the frame
rules.noHatSunglasses: you are not wearing a hat or sunglasses

inform.inset: Remember this is a probation check in and should be treated the same as a face-to-face meeting. You should wear appropriate clothing when taking the video.

record.title: Record your video
record.intro: You do not need to do anything during the video. Anything you say will not be recorded.
record.permissionsHeading: You need to accept permissions on your device
record.permissionsBody: We are unable to access your camera to record a video. Check your browser settings and permissions. If you are having trouble using this service, contact your probation officer.
record.browserUnsupported: Your browser does not support video

view.title: View your video

match.heading: We have confirmed this is you
match.intro: We have confirmed this is you. You can now continue to check your answers.

noMatch.heading: We cannot confirm this is you
noMatch.intro: We have checked and we cannot confirm this is you. You can try recording again or submit your video anyway.

rules.litArea: you are in a well lit area
rules.plainBackground: you are in front of a plain background
rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the frame
rules.noHatSunglasses: you are not wearing a hat or sunglasses

view.browserUnsupported: Your browser does not support video

index.heading: Check in with your probation officer
index.intro: You can use this service to check in with your probation officer as part of your probation. Your probation officer will have signed you up for this service during a face-to-face appointment.

beforeStart.heading: Before you start

items.details: your name and date of birth

beforeStart.uk: You will not be able to use this service to check in if you are outside of the UK.

whatToDo.heading: What you need to do
whatToDo.intro: As part of this service you will need to:

items.questions: answer a set of questions relating to how you are and if you need any help
items.camera: give permission to access your device’s camera
items.verify: verify your identity by moving your face into a frame on your screen using your camera, and following instructions on the screen

whatToDo.comparison: We will compare an image from you verifying your identity with the photo your probation officer took when they signed you up. This will not use your microphone or location.
whatToDo.shareAnswers: The answers to your questions will be sent to your probation officer to review.

help.heading: If you need help
help.intro: If you have difficulty using this service, or you do not have access to everything you need, you should contact your probation officer and let them know.
help.guidance: You can learn more about online check ins in our guidance.

inform.title: Confirm your identity
inform.intro: Next you need to verify your identity by moving your face into a frame on your screen using your camera. Follow the instructions to move your face closer, keeping it in the frame.
inform.comparison: We’ll compare an image from this with the photo your probation officer took when they signed you up. This will only be used to confirm your identity.
inform.permissions: You’ll need to accept permissions to access your camera on your device.
inform.rulesIntro: Before you verify your identity, make sure:

rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the frame
rules.litArea: you are in a well lit area
rules.plainBackground: you are in front of a plain background
rules.noHatSunglasses: you are not wearing a hat or sunglasses

inform.inset: Remember this is a probation check in and should be treated the same as a face-to-face meeting. You should wear appropriate clothing.

match.heading: We have confirmed this is you
match.intro: We have confirmed this is you. You can now continue to check your answers.

liveNoMatch.heading: We cannot confirm this is you
liveNoMatch.intro: This is because your image does not match the photo we have on your file.
liveNoMatch.rulesHeading: You can try to verify your identity again

rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the frame
rules.litArea: you are in a well lit area
rules.plainBackground: you are in front of a plain background
rules.noHatSunglasses: you are not wearing a hat or sunglasses

notLiveMatch.heading: We cannot confirm your identity
notLiveMatch.intro: We cannot confirm this is you, as you did not move quickly enough, or your face left the frame.
notLiveMatch.rulesHeading: If you try to verify your identity again

rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the frame
rules.followInstructions: you follow the instructions to move closer, keeping your face in the frame
rules.steadyPace: you move at a steady pace

notLiveNoMatch.heading: There's been a problem with your identity check
notLiveNoMatch.intro: We cannot confirm this is you. This is because:

reasons.noMatch: your image does not match the photo we have on your file
reasons.notLive: you did not move quickly enough, or your face left the frame

notLiveNoMatch.rulesHeading: If you try to verify your identity again

rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the frame
rules.followInstructions: you follow the instructions to move closer, keeping your face in the frame
rules.steadyPace: you move at a steady pace

fallbackMatch.heading: We have confirmed this is you
fallbackMatch.intro: We have confirmed this is you. You can now continue to check your answers.

fallback.heading: We cannot confirm this is you
fallback.intro: We have checked and we cannot confirm this is you. You can try again or submit anyway.
fallback.rulesHeading: Before you try again, make sure:

rules.litArea: you are in a well lit area
rules.plainBackground: you are in front of a plain background
rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the frame
rules.noHatSunglasses: you are not wearing a hat or sunglasses

instructions.intro: To verify your identity you will need to:

items.placeFace: place your face in the frame
items.followInstructions: follow the instructions to move your face closer, keeping it in the frame

cameraError.heading: Verify your identity
cameraError.subHeading: You need to accept permissions on your device
cameraError.body: We are unable to access your camera to verify your identity. Check your browser settings and permissions. If you are having trouble using this service, contact your probation officer.

cameraFramerate.heading: There's been a problem with your identity check
cameraFramerate.intro: This is because your camera is working slowly when we’re trying to verify your identity.
cameraFramerate.advice: You could try closing any apps that may be slowing down your camera and trying to verify your identity again.
cameraFramerate.rulesHeading: If you try to verify your identity again

rules.steadyPace: you move at a steady pace

cancelled.intro: This is because you cancelled the check before it was finished. You can try to verify your identity again.

rules.steadyPace: you move at a steady pace

rules.steadyPace: you move at a steady pace

error.intro: We cannot confirm this is you due to an error. You can try to verify your identity again or record a video instead.

inform.title: Confirm your identity
inform.intro: Next, we will use your camera to confirm your identity. Keep your face in the centre of the screen and we will check it matches the photo your probation officer took. You do not need to speak, and nothing you say is recorded.
inform.comparison: We will save an image of you taken from the video to compare with the photo your probation officer took when they signed you up. This  will only be used to confirm your identity.
inform.permissions: You'll need to accept permissions to record a video on your device.
inform.rulesIntro: Before you record your video, make sure:

rules.litArea: you are in a well lit area
rules.plainBackground: you are in front of a plain background
rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the frame
rules.noHatSunglasses: you are not wearing a hat or sunglasses

inform.inset: Remember this is a probation check in and should be treated the same as a face-to-face meeting. You should wear appropriate clothing.

record.title: Confirm your identity
record.intro: Keep your face in the centre of the screen. We will check it matches the photo your probation officer took. This may take a few moments and you do not need to press anything.
record.permissionsHeading: You need to accept permissions on your device
record.permissionsBody: We are unable to access your camera. Check your browser settings and permissions. If you are having trouble using this service, contact your probation officer.
record.searching: Looking for you. Move your face into the centre of the screen.
record.hold: Hold still. We can see you.

guidance.noFace: We cannot see your face. Move so your face is in view of the camera.
guidance.moveLeft: Move your face to the left.
guidance.moveRight: Move your face to the right.
guidance.moveBack: Move further away from the camera.

partials.capped: You have tried several times. You can submit anyway, or contact your probation officer for help.

match.heading: We have confirmed this is you
match.intro: We have confirmed this is you. You can now continue to check your answers.

timeoutNoMatch.heading: We cannot confirm this is you
timeoutNoMatch.intro: We checked but could not confirm this is you. You can try again or submit anyway.

rules.litArea: you are in a well lit area
rules.plainBackground: you are in front of a plain background
rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the centre of the screen
rules.noHatSunglasses: you are not wearing a hat or sunglasses

timeoutNoFace.heading: We could not see you clearly
timeoutNoFace.intro: We could not get a clear view of your face in time. You can try again or submit anyway.
timeoutNoFace.rulesIntro: When you try again, make sure:

rules.litArea: you are in a well lit area, with light on your face
rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the centre of the screen
rules.holdStill: you hold the device steady and hold still
rules.noHatSunglasses: you are not wearing a hat or sunglasses

error.heading: We cannot confirm this is you because an error occurred
error.intro: Something went wrong. You can try again.
error.rulesIntro: When you try again, make sure:

rules.litArea: you are in a well lit area
rules.plainBackground: you are in front of a plain background
rules.faceInFrame: your face is in the centre of the screen
rules.noHatSunglasses: you are not wearing a hat or sunglasses

date.mustBeFutureOrToday: {{labelSentence}} must be today or in the future

index.title: Probation Check-in Service
index.intro: This service allows you to check in with your probation officer remotely.

howToAccess.heading: How to access this service
howToAccess.body: To use this service, you need a unique link provided by your probation officer. If you have not received a link, please contact your probation officer.
howToAccess.inset: If you have received a link to check in, please use that link to access the service.

needHelp.heading: Need help?
needHelp.body: If you are having difficulty accessing this service, contact your probation officer for assistance.

purpose.heading: Purpose
purpose.intro: This privacy notice explains the standards that you can expect from the Probation Service

items.personalData: when we request, use or hold personal information (‘personal data’) about you
items.access: how you can get access to a copy of your personal data
items.complain: how you can complain if you think we have done something wrong

purpose.hmpps: The Probation Service is one part of Her Majesty’s Prison and Probation Service (HMPPS) which is part of the Ministry of Justice (MoJ).
purpose.collect: We need to collect your personal information to carry out functions such as delivering sentences given by the courts, in custody and the community, and rehabilitating people in our care through education and employment.

definitions.heading: Definitions
definitions.controller: The data controller is the decision maker where personal data is being processed. They decide why personal data is being processed and how it will be processed.
definitions.personalData: Personal data or personal information is any information that can be used to identify a natural person, either directly or indirectly.
definitions.moj: The MoJ is the data controller for the personal information we hold in HMPPS.

types.heading: Types of personal data we process
types.intro: We only process personal data that is relevant for the check in service we are providing to you.

items.feelings: information you give us about how you are feeling

purposeProcessing.heading: Purpose of processing and the lawful basis for the process
purposeProcessing.legalBasis: The legal basis for processing your personal information is that it is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or the exercise of official authority vested in us.
purposeProcessing.decisions: Your personal information that we collect is used to help us make decisions about you and your progress.
purposeProcessing.lawfulDuty: We also have a lawful duty to prevent crime, execute criminal penalties and safeguarding against the prevention of threats to public security, and the collection and use of your personal information is also necessary to enable us to comply with our obligations under:

use.heading: How we will use your information
use.body: We’ll use the information you give us as part of your check in to update your probation officer about how you are doing, and if you need any help. This is stored in our system securely.

sharing.heading: Who the information may be shared with
sharing.intro: From time to time, we may involve other organisations who can help you further. We may need to pass some information to:
sharing.tellBefore: We will normally tell you before passing your personal information onto others; however, in some cases (such as safeguarding cases) we may not need to tell you, but this will follow data protection laws.

askingForData.heading: When we ask you for personal data
askingForData.intro: We promise to tell you why we need your personal data and will only ask for what we need. You can:

items.refuse: refuse to agree to share information at any time, where it is legal to do so
items.discuss: discuss with your probation officer if you have any questions or concerns
items.protect: protect your information and make sure no unauthorised person has access to it

askingForData.possession: If you have personal information in your possession and are worried about keeping it safe, please speak to your probation officer.

promises.shareOnlyIfNeeded: only share your personal information with other organisations if we need to and it’s relevant
promises.notKeepTooLong: make sure we don’t keep your personal information longer than we need to
promises.notForSale: not make your personal information available for sale to others
promises.considerRequest: consider your request to correct, stop collecting or delete your personal information if relevant and legal

askingForData.survey: Occasionally we may use your contact information to ask you if you would like to complete a survey, or take part in research about online check ins. We use this information to see where and how we could improve our services to you.
askingForData.moreInfo: If you want more information on how we use and store your data, read the Probation Service Privacy Notice and the HMPPS Records, Information Management and Retention Policy.

retention.heading: How long we keep your information
retention.policy: We have a detailed policy for how long we need to keep your personal information. You can speak to your probation officer about this or check the HMPPS Records, information management and management retention policy.
retention.details: You can get more details about:

items.agreements: agreements we have with other organisations for sharing information
items.instructions: our instructions to staff on how to collect and manage your personal information
items.checkAccurate: how we check that the information we hold is accurate and up to date

retention.moreInfo: For more information, speak to your probation officer.

access.heading: Access to your personal information
access.body: You can find out if we hold any personal information about you by speaking to your probation officer. They will be able to give you some routine information, which doesn’t fall under a subject access request (SAR)

sar.heading: Subject access requests (SAR)
sar.body: If you would like a copy of all the information we hold about you, your probation officer can email the team and ask to see it. This is called a Subject Access Request and means that you can see what information we hold about you. You can ask your probation officer and they’ll submit this on your behalf.
sar.contact: You can also contact data.access1@justice.gov.uk to ask for a copy of the information.

contact.heading: Contact details and Data Protection Officer
contact.onBehalf: On behalf of the MoJ Data Protection Officer.
contact.dpo: You can also contact the Ministry of Justice Data Protection Officer at DPO@justice.gov.uk if you have any questions or concerns about how we process your information.

complaints.heading: Complaints
complaints.body: When we ask you for information, we will keep to the law. If you consider that your personal information has been handled incorrectly, you can contact the Information Commissioner for independent advice about data protection. You can contact the Information Commissioner at:
complaints.address:
Information Commissioner's Office
Wycliffe House
Water Lane
Wilmslow
Cheshire
SK9 5AF
Tel: 0303 123 1113 http://www.ico.org.uk

accessibility.title: Accessibility statement for Check in with your probation officer
accessibility.applies: This accessibility statement applies to the Check in with your probation officer service.
accessibility.runBy: This website is run by Ministry of Justice (MOJ) We want as many people as possible to be able to use this website. For example, that means you should be able to:

items.colours: change colours, contrast levels and fonts using browser or device settings
items.zoom: zoom in up to 400% without the text spilling off the screen
items.keyboard: navigate most of the website using a keyboard or speech recognition software
items.screenReader: listen to most of the website using a screen reader (including the most recent versions of JAWS, NVDA and VoiceOver)

accessibility.simple: We’ve also made the website text as simple as possible to understand.
accessibility.abilityNet: AbilityNet has advice on making your device easier to use if you have a disability.

howAccessible.heading: How accessible this website is
howAccessible.intro: We know some parts of this website are not fully accessible:

items.video: the video feature can cause challenges for people with low or no vision
items.speechToText: some users using speech to text might have difficulties navigating some fields
items.backLinks: some back links do not navigate to the expected change, possibly causing users to get lost in the service
items.hiddenText: some visually hidden text and labels need to be updated as they contain unique IDs making them harder to understand
items.headings: some pages have heading structures which could be confusing

feedback.heading: Feedback and contact information
feedback.body: If you find any problems not listed on this page or think we’re not meeting accessibility requirements, contact the service team at: {{supportEmailAddress}}
feedback.respond: We’ll consider your request and get back to you in 5 working days.

enforcement.heading: Enforcement procedure
enforcement.body: The Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC) is responsible for enforcing the Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018 (the ‘accessibility regulations’). If you’re not happy with how we respond to your complaint, contact the Equality Advisory and Support Service (EASS).

technical.heading: Technical information about this website’s accessibility
technical.body: The Ministry of Justice (MOJ) is committed to making its website accessible, in accordance with the Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018.

compliance.heading: Compliance status
compliance.tested: This service will be tested by 30 May 2026.
compliance.publish: We will publish a full accessibility statement by 30 June 2026.
compliance.notCompliant: This service is not compliant with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines version 2.1 AA standard. The non-compliances are listed below.
compliance.notTested: This service has not yet been tested.

outsideScope.heading: Content that’s not within the scope of the accessibility regulations
outsideScope.body: We have no content outside the scope of accessibility regulations.

preparation.heading: Preparation of this accessibility statement
preparation.body: This statement was prepared on 15 April 2026.

cookies.title: Cookies policy for Check in with your probation officer
cookies.heading: Cookies policy for Check in with your probation officer
cookies.intro: The Check in with your probation officer service stores small pieces of data (known as 'cookies') on your computer in order to operate, and to collect information about how you use online check ins.

items.measure: measure how you use the sites, so they can be updated and improved based on your needs

cookies.thirdParty: Third-party cookies are not used to identify you personally.

session.heading: Session cookies
session.intro: The Check in with your probation officer service may store a cookie when you log in to open a session. The cookies used are classed as essential cookies. This means they are necessary to provide this online service, where they are used to transmit, carry out or facilitate the transmission of communications over a network.

table.caption: Types of session cookies used within service

session.name: hmpps-esupervision-ui.session
session.purpose: This is used to manage user authentication and session data. This is essential to make online check ins work.

featureFlags.name: es-feature-flags
featureFlags.purpose: This is a functional cookie that controls what functions are available to you.

lang.name: lang
lang.purpose: Remembers your preferred language (English or Welsh)

analytics.heading: Analytics cookies
analytics.body: We do not use any analytics, tracking or marketing cookies in online check ins.
analytics.privacyLink: You can check our privacy policy to learn more about how your data is used.

guidance.title: About the Check in with your probation officer service
guidance.intro: This service will allow you to check in with your probation officer online. This could be used in between your in-person appointments, or to replace some of your in-person appointments.

choosing.heading: Choosing if online check ins are right for you
choosing.talk: Your probation officer will talk with you about how online check ins work, and you can decide together if you’d like to use them or if they’re something that’s suitable for you.
choosing.stop: You can stop online check ins at any time by talking to your probation officer.
choosing.accessible: We are working to make these online check ins as accessible as possible. We will continue this work to fix any accessibility issues that come up.

signup.heading: How you’ll be signed up to use online check ins
signup.intro: If you choose to use online check ins, you’ll be signed up by your probation officer. They may do this in your in-person appointment. To sign up, they will need to enter:

items.name: your name and date of birth
items.phone: your phone number or email address
items.photo: a photograph of your face we can use to match your identity
items.startDate: the date you will start using online check ins
items.frequency: how often you will use online check ins

signup.confirm: Once you’ve been signed up, you’ll get a message to your preferred contact method to confirm you have signed up and telling you when you’ll get a link to complete your first check in online.

photoMatch.heading: How we use your photo to match with your submissions
photoMatch.body: We take a photo of you during sign up to match with a video you’ll take during your check in. This is to make sure it’s you completing a check in, just like some banking apps ask for a photo or video to check you’re the right person. Your probation officer may take this or ask you to take it and send it to them.

completing.heading: How you will complete a check in

getLink.heading: Getting the link to use online check ins
getLink.firstCheckIn: When your probation officer signs you up to use online check ins, they will set a date for your first check in.
getLink.messageDate: On this date, you’ll get a message either by text or email, whichever one you chose. This will have a link to check in, and you’ll have 3 days to complete it.

whatAsked.heading: What you'll be asked to do
whatAsked.intro: During your check in you’ll be asked:

items.name: your name and date of birth
items.questions: up to 5 questions about how you are and if you need support with anything

whatAsked.treat: You should treat your online check in like any other probation appointment and make sure you’re fully and appropriately dressed.
whatAsked.outsideUk: You will not be able to use this service to check in if you are outside of the UK.

verify.heading: How we verify your identity
verify.compare: An image of you verifying your identity will be compared to the photo of you taken when you signed up. This is to confirm it is you completing the check in. You’ll also need to verify your identity by moving your face into a frame on your screen using your camera. Follow the instructions to move your face closer, keeping it in the frame.
verify.permissions: You’ll need to accept permissions to access your camera on your device.

cannotConfirm.heading: If we cannot confirm your identity
cannotConfirm.continue: If we cannot confirm your identity, you can still choose to continue and submit your check in anyway. A failed identity check can be caused by lots of things, such as if you’ve changed how you look, if you are not in a well lit area or if you’ve not moved quickly enough in front of the camera.
cannotConfirm.review: Your probation officer will compare your identity check to your photo when they review your check in. If they have any questions, they will contact you.

questions.heading: Questions in your online check in
questions.intro: During your online check in, you’ll be asked some questions. There are 2 questions that everyone is asked at every check in:

items.feeling: How have you been feeling since we last spoke?
items.support: Is there anything you need support with or want to let us know about?

questions.extra: Your probation officer can then choose up to 3 extra questions to ask you. These questions will be about you, your life and anything you’ve been talking with your probation officer about. They’ll choose what to ask you to make sure you can give a proper update to them. This means they’ll be able to help you with things in a better way, and ask about things specific to you and your needs.

confirmation.heading: Confirmation that you’ve completed the check in and what happens next
confirmation.body: Once you’ve submitted your check in, you’ll get a text or an email to confirm that you’ve completed a check in and that your probation officer will review it and get in touch if needed.

askedSupport.heading: If you have asked for support
askedSupport.body: If you have asked for support with something in your check in, or said that you are not feeling great or you’re struggling, your probation officer will get in touch with you to see what they can do to help.

failed.heading: If you fail to check in
failed.body: If you fail to check in for any reason, your probation officer may stop your check ins and contact you to find out why you’ve not been able to complete a check in.
failed.difficulty: If you’re having difficulty using online check ins, you should let your probation officer know as soon as possible so they can stop check ins.

struggling.heading: What to do if you’re struggling or something goes wrong
struggling.intro: Contact your probation officer or speak to them in your face-to-face appointment if you have any issues with online check ins. This could include:

items.difficult: you're finding it difficult to use
items.lostPhone: you've lost your phone or have an issue accessing the link
items.faceToFace: you want to go back to face-to-face only

struggling.noBreach: Any issues you have with online check ins will not result in a breach of your conditions.

info.heading: How we will use your information
info.body: We’ll use the information you give us as part of your check in to update your probation officer about how you are doing, and if you need any support. This is stored in our system securely.
info.privacyLink: You can check our privacy policy to learn more about how we use your data.

sar.heading: Subject access requests
sar.body: If you would like a copy of the information we hold about you, your probation officer can email the team and ask to see it. This is called a subject access request. You can ask your probation officer and they’ll submit this on your behalf.
sar.email: You can also email the team at: data.access1@justice.gov.uk to ask for a copy of the information.

feedback.heading: How to give feedback about online check ins
feedback.form: We have an online feedback form for you to tell us your experience of online check ins.
feedback.share: You can fill in the form yourself or with your probation officer whenever you have something to share about online check ins.
feedback.other: There’ll be other opportunities for you to give feedback, for example, you could choose to talk about your experience in short phone calls.

practitionerGuidance.title: About online check ins
practitionerGuidance.headerServiceLabel: Manage probation check ins Private Beta
practitionerGuidance.intro: Online check ins allow people on probation to update you about their needs and how they are feeling. They’ll do this by answering some questions about how they are and if they need any support. We’ll then ask them to verify their identity to confirm it’s them completing the check in. Once they have completed their check in, you’ll review their answers and can use this information to help monitor risk.

meaning.heading: What online check ins mean for you
meaning.body: It is an organisational decision to pilot this tool, as part of wider package of digital products designed to support the Probation Service. We have guidance relating to who is eligible and suitable for online check ins to help you decide who on your caseload can use the service. In line with existing practice, any decisions relating to the frequency of reporting must be recorded.

howUse.heading: How you can use online check ins
howUse.intro: You can use online check ins:

items.replace: to replace some face-to-face contact where appropriate (option 1)
items.addition: as well as existing face-to-face contact to give you an update between appointments (option 2)

howUse.notAll: Not everyone will be eligible or suitable to use online check ins, and some might only be eligible for option 2 – using online check ins in addition to existing face-to-face appointments.

notEligible.heading: Who is not eligible to use online check ins for either option
notEligible.body: People on the Intensive Supervision Court pilot are not eligible for online check ins.

replace.heading: Using online check ins to replace some face-to-face contact (option 1)
replace.monthly: If you’re meeting someone in person every month but they’re doing well, you could use online check ins every other month in place of a face-to-face appointment. While using online check ins in this way, you can deviate from national standards about monthly face-to-face meetings.
replace.judgement: Once you’ve determined if the person is eligible, you should use your judgement to decide if they’re suitable.
replace.suitable: Someone may be suitable to use online check ins to replace some face-to-face contact if they have:

items.stable: stable lifestyle factors (for example, employment, accommodation, relationships)
items.time: spent time on probation and understand their responsibilities
items.relationship: an established relationship with their probation practitioner
items.agreed: agreed to use online check ins
items.digital: sufficient digital literacy to complete online forms
items.phone: access to a mobile phone or technology they can use the internet on
items.noRestrictions: no restrictions on device or internet use as part of their conditions
items.noDependencies: no dependencies in their sentence plan that could be affected by using online check ins

replace.someOnly: You must only replace some face-to-face meetings. You cannot use online check ins to replace all face-to-face contact with a person.
replace.approval: All decisions to use an online check in to reduce contact must be made with the approval of a senior probation officer, and will have managerial oversight.

excluded.heading: Who is excluded from using online check ins to replace some face-to-face contact (option 1)
excluded.intro: People are not eligible to use online check ins to replace face-to-face contact if they are:

items.suspended: on suspended supervision (for example, through IMPACT and Reset)
items.highRisk: at very high risk of serious harm (ROSH)
items.mappa: a MAPPA (multi-agency public protection arrangements) eligible case level 2 and 3
items.child: part of a child protection or child in need plan
items.domesticAbuse: under a current domestic abuse conviction, have a risk flag for domestic abuse or other evidence that could suggest domestic abuse

addition.heading: Using online check ins in addition to existing face-to-face contact (option 2)
addition.extra: If you’re meeting a person on probation every two weeks and would like an extra contact due to concern about them, using online check ins could give you an update about how they are without needing an additional face-to-face appointment.
addition.suitable: Someone may be suitable to use online check ins as well as existing face-to-face appointments if:

items.additional: you would like additional information about them for risk management
items.agree: they agree to use online check ins
items.digital: sufficient digital literacy to complete online forms
items.phone: access to a mobile phone or technology they can use the internet on
items.noRestrictions: they have no restrictions on device or internet use as part of their conditions
items.noDependencies: no dependencies in their sentence plan that could be affected by using online check ins

tailored.heading: Tailored questions to help monitor risk
tailored.intro: All online check ins will include two questions:

items.feeling: How have you been feeling since we last spoke?
items.support: Is there anything you need support with or want to let us know about?

tailored.additional: In addition to these two questions, you can choose to add up to 3 additional questions, up to 23:59 the day before the check in link is sent. You’ll choose from a list of question starters, which all have a space for you to add the subject you want. These are then added to the person’s check in, following the order you add them to the list.

examples.heading: Examples of tailored questions
examples.detail: Some questions have a bit more detail, asking if someone has been able to do something, or get in touch with a person or organisation. Others are more open, allowing you to add more detail or something more specific to their circumstances.
examples.list: You’ll choose from a list of question starters, which all have a space for you to customise. For example, you can change 'Have you been able to [insert your text here]?' to 'Have you been able to speak with your GP about your health?.

writing.heading: How to write questions for online services

consider.heading: Things to consider
consider.different: Online check ins are very different to face-to-face appointments, as they remove the personal connection and can feel quite detached for people on probation.
consider.reluctant: We’ve also heard from people on probation that they are reluctant to write some things down as they are concerned it could be used against them.

shouldDo.heading: What you should do when writing questions for online check ins
shouldDo.think: Think about how the question will come across to someone reading it without the context of a face-to-face conversation.

items.appropriate: consider if a question is appropriate for an online service
items.context: think about the person’s circumstances when asking questions about topics that could be upsetting or dangerous, such as drugs, alcohol or abusive relationships
items.single: avoid asking about more than one topic in a single question
items.simple: avoid using complicated language or words that have a simpler alternative

data.heading: How data is used and who can see it
data.use: We’ll use the information the person submits to update you about how they are doing, and if they need any support. This is stored in our system securely.
data.speak: They should speak to you about anything relating to their data, and you can email the team at checkinwithprobation@justice.gov.uk so we can action any data requests.
data.privacyLink: You can check our privacy policy to learn more about how we use data.

sar.heading: Subject access requests
sar.body: If anyone you have signed up to use online check ins would like a copy of their information, you can submit a subject access request on their behalf.
sar.email: You can also email the team at: data.access1@justice.gov.uk to ask for a copy of the information.

stop.heading: When online check ins stop
stop.body: If a person on probation’s sentence ends, including where Early Termination is approved, their online check ins will stop automatically. Practitioners do not need to stop check ins manually or record the person as not eligible.

wantStop.heading: If someone wants to stop using online check ins
wantStop.body: If a person on your caseload wants to stop using online check ins, you can stop their check ins which will stop the links being sent. This will not delete any data and you will still be able to access past check ins. If they decide to restart, you can choose to restart check ins.

timeOff.heading: What happens if you take time off
timeOff.intro: If you have annual leave or other leave which means you’ll be unable to review completed check ins, you should talk to the practitioner who will be covering your cases and tell them when the person is expected to check in.
timeOff.notifications: They will not receive notifications for the person, so will need to search for them in Manage people on probation to:

items.find: find out if they’ve checked in
items.followUp: carry out any follow up tasks needed

timeOff.feedback: We would like to hear your feedback about how this works and what could be improved.

contactChange.heading: If someone’s contact details change
contactChange.body: You can view and change a person’s contact details in Manage people on probation.

concerns.heading: If you have concerns about someone’s risk
concerns.body: If you have concerns about new or escalating risk for someone on your caseload, you should speak to your Senior Probation Officer (SPO) to decide what to do next. You might want to stop their online check ins. The person would then revert back to in-person appointments only.

wantStop.heading: If someone wants to stop using online check ins
wantStop.body: If a person on your caseload wants to stop using online check ins, you can stop their check ins which will stop the links being sent. This will not delete any data and you will still be able to access past check ins. If they decide to restart, you’ll have to set them up again. This will cause a duplication in the system. This is something we’re working on and your feedback is appreciated.

failed.heading: If someone fails to check in
failed.intro: There could be many reasons why someone fails to check in, including if:

items.contactChanged: their contact details have changed and they have not yet updated you
items.lostAccess: they lose access to their phone or email
items.notDelivered: the link to check in is not delivered
items.wrong: something has gone wrong for them

whatToDo.heading: What to do if someone fails to check in
whatToDo.intro: You should use your discretion when dealing with failed check ins, but you could:

items.contact: contact the person to find out why they have failed to check in
items.stop: stop check ins so the person would revert to in-person appointments only
items.raise: raise any issues with contacting the person through the usual channels
items.speak: speak to the single point of contact for online check ins in your office, or your senior probation officer

whatToDo.notLinked: For now, online check ins will not be linked to the person’s conditions, and there will be no breaches. You can use your judgement depending on the situation. If someone hasn’t checked in and there is not a reason for this, you could:

options.warning: give them a verbal warning after the first failed check in
options.stopAfterSecond: stop check ins after a second failure to complete an online check in and revert to face-to-face contact only

mailbox.heading: Support mailbox
mailbox.intro: Use the checkinwithprobation@justice.gov.uk mailbox to tell us if:

items.notWorking: online check ins are not working
items.notAsExpected: something in online check ins is not working as expected
items.struggling: someone you manage is struggling to use online check ins and needs support
items.delete: someone asks for their data to be deleted
items.sar: someone submits a subject access request (SAR) under the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA)

thoughts.heading: Tell us your thoughts
thoughts.body: Whether you’ve been using online check ins regularly or haven’t been able to get started yet, we’d really appreciate hearing from you using our survey, which takes about 5 minutes.

monthly.heading: Join our monthly feedback calls
monthly.meetings: Each month we will be hosting 1-hour Teams meetings where you can discuss your experiences of using online check ins with us. These meetings will be open to staff from different offices so you can also learn about the experiences of others. You can attend as many or as few feedback calls as you wish.
monthly.form: If you are new to online check ins or have been involved in the first phase, we need you to complete this short form to receive an invitation to provide feedback. By filling out the form, you’ll confirm that you’d like to provide feedback and understand how we’ll handle your information.

caseload.heading: Feedback from people on your caseload
caseload.form: We have a feedback form for people using online check ins. This is to understand their experiences.
caseload.access: They will be able to access this:

items.link: using a link to it within the confirmation notification they receive when they’ve checked in

caseload.complete: You can also complete this with them in face-to-face appointments or share it with them if they want to complete this themselves.
caseload.other: There may be other ways we contact people on probation to hear their feedback. For example, conducting short phone calls from the local Engaging People on Probation (EPOP) staff member to learn about their experience.
caseload.update: We will update this guidance when the new form is available.

whatWeDo.heading: What we do with your feedback
whatWeDo.body: If something in online check ins is not working or online check ins go down, your feedback will enable us to fix these issues as soon as possible. Your feedback will also help us to improve the design and the way it works.

notFound.heading: Page not found
notFound.typed: If you typed the web address, check it is correct.
notFound.pasted: If you pasted the web address, check you copied the entire address.
notFound.linkOrButton: If the web address is correct or you selected a link or button, contact your probation officer.

authError.heading: Authorisation error
authError.body: You are not authorised to use this application.

outsideUk.title: Sorry, the service is not available outside of the UK
outsideUk.body: You cannot use this service if you are currently outside of the UK. Contact your probation officer for help.

provide.heading: Give feedback about online check ins
provide.optional: You can answer these questions if you want to give feedback on using online check ins to help us improve it. This is completely optional.

howEasy.legend: How easy was it to use online check ins?
howEasy.error: Select how easy it was to use online check ins

gettingSupport.legend: Are you getting the support you need from probation? (optional)

improvements.legend: What part of online check ins could we improve? (optional)

thankyou.heading: Thank you for your feedback
thankyou.body: Your feedback is anonymous so you will not receive a reply.
